days.title: Arrival in Lisbon
days.subtitle: Exploring the vibrant capital

timeline.title: Arrive at Lisbon Airport
timeline.detail: Arrive at Lisbon Humberto Delgado Airport. Follow signs to the metro station for a smart transport choice to the city.

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Lisboa Plaza
timeline.detail: Check into this charming hotel located in the heart of the city, just a 10-minute walk from the nearest metro station.

timeline.title: Lunch at Time Out Market
timeline.detail: Enjoy a variety of delicious dishes from local chefs at this vibrant food market. Sample everything from fresh seafood to traditional custard tarts.

timeline.title: Visit Belém Tower
timeline.detail: Explore this iconic fortress, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and learn about its historical significance in Portugal’s Age of Discoveries.

timeline.title: Stroll through Alfama District
timeline.detail: Wander the narrow streets of this historic neighborhood, known for its medieval architecture and stunning viewpoints of the city.

timeline.title: Dinner at A Cevicheria
timeline.detail: Dine at this trendy restaurant serving Peruvian ceviche with a Portuguese twist, highly praised for its fresh and innovative flavors.

localTips: Buy a Lisboa Card for unlimited travel on public transport and free entry to many attractions.
For the best photos of Belém Tower, visit early or late in the day to avoid crowds.
Try the iconic Pastéis de Belém — the original custard tarts — just a short walk from the tower.
Keep an eye on your belongings in crowded areas, especially in popular tourist spots.
Explore the vibrant street art scene in the Bairro Alto district.

days.title: Lisbon Highlights and Culture
days.subtitle: Diving deeper into the city's treasures

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel Lisboa Plaza
timeline.detail: Enjoy a complimentary breakfast featuring a variety of fresh pastries, fruits, and local dishes to start your day right.

timeline.title: Visit the Jerónimos Monastery
timeline.detail: Discover the stunning architecture of this UNESCO World Heritage Site and the resting place of legendary explorer Vasco da Gama.

timeline.title: Lunch at O Prego da Peixaria
timeline.detail: Indulge in a unique twist on the classic prego sandwich, combining flavors from the sea with fresh ingredients. A local favorite.
timeline.mapQuery: O Prego da Peixaria, Lisbon, Portugal

timeline.title: Explore the National Azulejo Museum
timeline.detail: Immerse yourself in the history of Portuguese tiles and admire stunning displays of azulejos from different eras and styles.

timeline.title: Take a Tram 28 Ride
timeline.detail: Experience the charms of Lisbon aboard the iconic Tram 28, winding through historic neighborhoods and providing stunning views.

timeline.title: Dinner at Ramiro
timeline.detail: Savor the finest seafood at this renowned local eatery, famous for its fresh shellfish and casual atmosphere. Be prepared for a wait!

localTips: Purchase a combined ticket for Jerónimos Monastery and the Maritime Museum for additional savings.
Be sure to arrive early at popular places like Ramiro to avoid long wait times.
Try the garlic shrimp and the cracked crab at Ramiro — their specialties!
Consider visiting the azulejo museum on a weekday for fewer crowds.
Use the tram or metro system to easily navigate between attractions.

days.title: Final Day in Lisbon
days.subtitle: Relaxing and reflecting

timeline.title: Breakfast at Fabrica Coffee Roasters
timeline.detail: Enjoy artisanal coffee and freshly baked pastries at this popular café, perfect for a cozy start to your last day.

timeline.title: Visit the Calouste Gulbenkian Museum
timeline.detail: Discover a vast collection of ancient and modern art at this world-class museum, set in a serene park setting.

timeline.title: Lunch at Mercado da Ribeira
timeline.detail: Enjoy a selection of gourmet street food options in this market hall. Perfect for tasting a variety of cuisines in one spot.

timeline.title: Relax at Eduardo VII Park
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely stroll through this beautiful park, enjoying the lush gardens and panoramic views over the city and the river.

timeline.title: Visit the Lisbon Oceanarium
timeline.detail: End your trip with an engaging visit to one of the largest aquariums in Europe, featuring an impressive variety of marine life.

timeline.title: Dinner at Bairro do Avillez
timeline.detail: Conclude your Lisbon experience with a gourmet dinner at this unique venue offering a variety of traditional Portuguese dishes.

localTips: Consider getting a day pass for metro and tram for unlimited travel on your last day.
Try local wines at Bairro do Avillez for an authentic culinary experience.
Plan your Oceanarium visit in the late afternoon to avoid crowds.
Bring a camera to capture the stunning views at Eduardo VII Park during sunset.
Visit local shops in neighborhoods like Chiado for unique souvenirs.

hotels.name: Hotel Lisboa Plaza
hotels.note: Central location, 10-minute walk from Avenida metro station.

budget.note: Access to public transport and attractions
